description: Mapping of repositories to CISA PPD-21 critical infrastructure sectors and emerging sectors relevant to cybersecurity research.

predicates.description: CISA PPD-21 designated critical infrastructure sectors.
Sectors not in PPD-21 but increasingly relevant to cybersecurity.

entry.description: Manufacturing, storing, and transporting chemical substances. SRMA: DHS.
Sites drawing large crowds for shopping, business, entertainment, or lodging. SRMA: DHS.
Infrastructure for telecommunications, broadcasting, cable, and internet services. SRMA: DHS.
Production of essential materials and goods like metals, machinery, and vehicles. SRMA: DHS.
Dam projects, navigation locks, levees, hurricane barriers, mine tailings, and water retention systems. SRMA: DHS.
Research, development, production, delivery, and maintenance of military weapons systems and components. SRMA: DOD.
Resources providing response services during and after incidents. SRMA: DHS.
Electricity, oil, and natural gas infrastructure. SRMA: DOE.
Banks, credit unions, insurance, securities, and investment institutions. SRMA: Treasury.
Farms, restaurants, and food manufacturing, processing, and storage. SRMA: USDA/HHS.
Buildings, grounds, and infrastructure owned by federal, state, local, tribal, or territorial governments. SRMA: DHS/GSA.
Public and private healthcare providers, health insurance, medical equipment, and public health systems. SRMA: HHS.
Hardware, software, IT systems, and services including internet infrastructure. SRMA: DHS.

entry.expanded: Nuclear Reactors, Materials, and Waste Sector
entry.description: Nuclear power plants, research and test reactors, fuel cycle facilities, radioactive waste. SRMA: DHS.
Aviation, highway, maritime, mass transit, pipeline, freight rail, and postal shipping. SRMA: DHS/DOT.
Drinking water and wastewater treatment and distribution systems. SRMA: EPA.
K-12 and higher education institutions. Increasingly targeted by ransomware and data theft.
Satellite infrastructure, ground stations, and space-based communications. Covered by SPD-5.
Cryptocurrency exchanges, DeFi protocols, blockchain infrastructure, and digital asset custody.
AI model hosting, training infrastructure, agent platforms, and ML pipelines as critical infrastructure.
